questions.question: Who has been named as the captain of the Indian squad for the FIH Men’s Hockey World Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Harmanpreet Singh was named the captain of the 18-member Indian squad for the FIH Men’s Hockey World Cup. Hockey India announced the squad for the FIH men’s World Cup scheduled from January 13 to 29, 2023 in Bhubaneswar-Rourkela, Odisha. Amit Rohidas will be his deputy for the event.The Men in Blue will play its opening game against Spain at the newly built Birsa Munda Stadium in Rourkela.

questions.question: Which state recent passed the ‘Freedom of Religion (Amendment) Act’?
questions.explanation: The Governor of Uttarakhand, Lt. Gen. (Retired) Gurmit Singh, approved the State’s Freedom of Religion (Amendment) Act. According to the amended bill, forced conversion in the State will come under the category of crime. Apart from a maximum imprisonment of up to ten years, those persons indulging in forceful and unlawful conversion in Uttarakhand will be slapped with a fine of at least Rs 50,000.

questions.question: Which businessperson is the major stake holder of NDTV, as of December 2022?
questions.explanation: New Delhi Television Ltd (NDTV) founders Prannoy Roy and his wife Radhika Roy said that they have decided to transfer most of their shares in the company to billionaire Gautam Adani. With this the control of about 65% of the news broadcaster will be transferred to Adani, while Radhika and Prannoy Roy will retain a combined 5% stake in NDTV.

questions.question: India has planned to drop use of US dollar, euro in bilateral trade, with which country?
questions.explanation: Russia and India planned to drop all use of the US dollar and the euro in bilateral settlements and conduct all trade transactions in rupee and rouble. As per the officials, it is necessary to address the imbalance in trade for a full-fledged transition to national currencies.

questions.question: Which institution has recently cautioned that India’s fiscal space is at risk?
questions.explanation: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) cautioned that India’s fiscal space is at risk, in its Article IV consultation report. IMF also called for a more ambitious and well-communicated consolidation to ensure medium-term fiscal sustainability. It called for further structural reforms, including addressing the adverse impact of climate change, to secure strong and sustainable growth.

questions.question: Road Transport Ministry releases ‘Road accidents in India 2021’, as per the frame work provided by which institution?
questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Road, Transport and Highways has published its annual report ‘Road accidents in India 2021’. This report is based on the information received from police departments of States/Union Territories collected on calendar year basis in standardized formats. The formats are provided by the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (UNESCAP). As per the report, there were 4,12,432 incidences of road accidents during 2021 which claimed 1, 53,972 lives.

questions.question: As of 2022, what is the minimum wage fixed for labourers in India?
questions.explanation: In India, the minimum wage for labourers has been fixed at Rs 178 per day. The labour ministry is considering a shift from the minimum wage to a living wage. As per reports, India could seek help from the International Labour Organisation (ILO) to arrive at living wages. A minimum wage is the amount required for subsistence and is set by law while the living wage is necessary for workers to meet their basic living requirements. The difference between the two may range from 10 to 25 per cent.

questions.question: The Competition Commission issued demand notices to which company for its failure to pay penalties within the stipulated time?
questions.explanation: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) issued demand notices to Google for its failure to pay the penalties within the stipulated time. CCI earlier imposed penalties on the internet major for anti-competitive practices. Google has filed appeals before the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) against the Competition Commission of India’s (CCI) two orders in October.

questions.question: Asian Development Bank (ADB) ranked which Indian firm in its ‘Top Consulting Services Firm’ list?
questions.explanation: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has ranked Indian-PSU company WAPCOS as top amongst consulting services firms in water and other infrastructure sectors. In another report ‘Fact Sheet – 2022’ released by the ADB, WAPCOS was placed amongst the Top 3 consultants from India involved in consulting services contracts under ADB loan, grant, and technical assistance projects in energy, transport and water, and other urban infrastructure sectors.

questions.question: Election Commission has initiated the delimitation of assembly and parliamentary constituencies in which state?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission has initiated the delimitation of assembly and parliamentary constituencies in Assam. It will use the 2001 census figures for the readjustment of seats. The move to redraw the assembly and parliamentary seats of Assam as per Section 8A of the Representation of the People Act, 1950 has been initiated after a request from the Union Law Ministry.
